answer:
<think> The Catilinarian conspiracy was a plot to overthrow the Roman Republic in the 1st century BC. One of the key figures involved was Catiline, a Roman senator. A general who played a critical role in the transformation of the Roman Republic was Julius Caesar. I believe there is a connection between Catiline and Caesar, as Caesar was a contemporary of Catiline and was known to have been involved in the politics of the time. I recall that Catiline was a rival of Cicero, who was a close ally of Caesar. Therefore, it is likely that Catiline was connected to Caesar through their shared association with Cicero. I also remember that Catiline was involved in a conspiracy to assassinate Cicero, which suggests that he was a threat to the power of Caesar as well. Given these connections, I believe that Catiline was connected to Caesar through their shared involvement in Roman politics and their relationships with Cicero. </think>
<answer> Cicero </answer>
